title: Newegg H1 2025: $695.7M Revenue, GPU Sales Drive 13% Growth
text:
Newegg H1 2025 Financial Performance Analysis.

GPU Sales & Growth Drivers: GPU sales were the primary engine of Newegg's first-half growth, fueled by launches of NVIDIA GeForce RTX 50 Series, AMD Radeon RX 9000 Series, and AMD Ryzen 9000X3D CPUs. The CEO stated these products boosted organic traffic and spurred robust cross-category purchasing.

Key Financial Metrics: Revenue Performance. Net sales: $695.7 million (12.6% YoY increase). GMV: $849.1 million (13.7% YoY growth). Gross profit: $79.8 million (26.5% YoY increase).

Profitability Turnaround: Net loss narrowed to $4.2 million from $25.0 million prior year. Adjusted EBITDA improved dramatically to $11.3 million versus negative $7.3 million.

Operational Highlights: Average order value increased to $467 from $401. Active customers grew to 1.13 million from 1.09 million. Repeat purchase rate improved to 25.2% from 23.0%.

Strategic Factors: Growth was supported by tariff-related pull-forward spending, SG&A expense reductions, and improved supply chain collaboration, demonstrating management's ability to navigate macroeconomic headwinds while capitalizing on high-demand product categories.
